ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning Fear.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Fear is divided into six varieties: viz., shrinking, shame, disgrace, consternation, panic, anxiety.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Shrinking is fear of some act about to take place.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Shame is fear arising from the anticipation of blame: and this is the highest form of the affection.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Disgrace is fear springing from some base act already done, and even for this form there is some hope of salvation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Consternation is fear originating in some huge product of the imagination.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Panic is fear caused by some unusual product of the imagination.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Anxiety is fear of failure, that is, of misfortune: for when we fear that our efforts will not meet with success, we suffer anxiety.
